title: Merge ladder RUNG 1: conflicted rebases whose transitive package footprints are disjoint compose with native gates only — plus global-zone and overlap-zone registries
summary: OPERATOR-PRIORITIZED (ladder rung 2 of 4). Depends on rung 0's composition-verdict ledger kind. When rebase required conflict resolution OR patch-id drifted, compute disjointness on the TRANSITIVE package import graph (go list ./... deps; Uber SubmitQueue build-target precedent — file paths are insufficient, merge skew is real): if the lane diff's package footprint and the audited_base..new_base delta's footprint do not intersect, compose + full native gates + composition-verdict{method:package-disjoint}. Two registries, policy-configured: GLOBAL ZONES always conflict (go.mod/go.sum, .evolve/policy.json, .claude/settings.json, hook wiring, generated projections like commands/*.md — regenerate at land via evolve skills generate, never merge); OVERLAP ZONES forbid composition entirely (trust-kernel packages: guards, ship, ledger => solo landing + full audit regardless). Include the Chromium flake screen: composed-tree test failure => rerun the failing test WITHOUT the lane patch; still failing => blame base, do not eject the lane.
acceptance: RED: TestPackageGraph_TransitiveNotFileLevel (file-disjoint but import-entangled => NOT disjoint)
go test -race PASS; apicover clean (graduate any new package)
